chief_complaint: A 40-year-old male presented with left-sided pleuritic chest pain.
history: The patient had no previous history of systemic disease, infection, or malignancy. Initial laboratory and imaging findings suggested exudative pleural effusion, but further investigations excluded infectious causes, tuberculosis, and malignancy. A detailed history and physical examination led to a suspicion of an autoimmune disease.
exam: Vital signs: normal. Physical examination revealed left-sided pleuritic chest pain and pleural effusion.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: ANA, ENA, anti-SSA (Ro) positive, Schirmer test positive. Imaging: pleural effusion, exudative type.
correct_diagnosis: Primary Sjogren's disease with pleural effusion
differentials: Primary Sjogren's disease with pleural effusion
correct_ranking: Primary Sjogren's disease with pleural effusion
